title: Opponent-window features focused on undercut/overcut context
summary: F1 strategy articles stress that pit calls depend on rival pressure, traffic windows, and whether new tyres can undercut or must avoid cold-tyre overcut loss.
rationale: F1 strategy articles stress that pit calls depend on rival pressure, traffic windows, and whether new tyres can undercut or must avoid cold-tyre overcut loss. The current best solution models only self-state; a cleaner set of race-context features could add the missing game-theory signal.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, add causal context features such as percentile rank/z-score of `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, count/share of cars currently on each compound, count of same-lap pitters, and position-bucket context (`P1-3`, `P4-10`, `11+`). Test only a compact set, not a wide noisy block.
expected_effect: Should add lift mainly in marginal decision zones where self tyre wear alone is ambiguous.
risk: Some field-context features can overfit specific races or duplicate noisy signals. Keep the feature set small and validate on held-out races.
